Respond with JSON only."},{"role":"user","content":"Find 3-4 alternatives for "{{name_en}}" available in North American stores.{% if reason %} User reason: {{reason}}.{% endif %}{% if taste %} User taste: {{taste}}.{% endif %}{% if profile_context %}{{profile_context}}{% endif %}
If a dietary restriction is given, EVERY alternative MUST be definitively compliant — no "if available" or "check label" items.
match levels (use strictly): "Very close" = same product, different brand (e.g., another brand of sriracha). Different product type = "Similar" at best. Plant-based for meat = "Different but works".
If user taste is specified, prioritize alternatives authentic to that cuisine.
ONLY JSON: {"note": "1 sentence why needed", "alts": [{"name_en": "Name", "match": "Very close|Similar|Different but works", "desc": "1 sentence", "where": "Where in store"}]}"}]

column1: sriracha — general alternative with no constraints
column10: The Flying Goose/Sambal Oelek attribution is factually wrong (Huy Fong makes Sambal Oelek; Flying Goose makes sriracha), which is a real product accuracy failure. The first 'alternative' is essentially the same product. Thai Kitchen Sweet Chili Sauce is rated 'Similar' but is substantially different from sriracha in sweetness and texture. These issues undermine the rubric's requirements for real products and honest match levels.
column12: {"note":"These options replace sriracha’s spicy, garlicky chili profile with products commonly found in North American grocery stores.","alts":[{"name_en":"Huy Fong Chili Garlic Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A classic chili-garlic hot sauce with a very similar heat, garlic, and tang to sriracha.","where":"Condiments aisle, hot sauce section"},{"name_en":"Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A straightforward sriracha-style sauce with the same sweet-heat profile and thick pourable texture.","where":"Condiments aisle, Asian sauces"},{"name_en":"Trader Joe's Sriracha Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A store-brand sriracha that closely matches the familiar garlic-chili flavor and use case.","where":"Condiments aisle, hot sauce section"},{"name_en":"Tabasco Sriracha Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A sriracha-style sauce that keeps the sweet, garlicky chili character while being slightly more vinegary.","where":"Condiments aisle, hot sauce section"}]}

column1: butter + lactose intolerant — all alts must be dairy-free
column10: The note acknowledges lactose intolerance, and all listed alternatives are presented as lactose-free or plant-based/lactose-free. However, the match levels are not fully honest per the rubric: the plant-based Country Crock option is labeled 'Different but works' rather than the requested caution that plant-based butters are 'Similar' not 'Very close' for baking. The first three are also questionable because they are conventional dairy butter products labeled as lactose-free, which may still count as dairy rather than dairy-free depending on interpretation, but they do address lactose-free specifically. Overall, the output does not fully satisfy the honesty requirement on match levels.
column12: {"note":"These options avoid lactose while covering common North American grocery-store uses for butter.","alts":[{"name_en":"Plant-based butter sticks","match":"Similar","desc":"Dairy-free butter-style sticks made from oils that work well for spreading, baking, and cooking.","where":"Refrigerated dairy or plant-based butter section"},{"name_en":"Olive oil spread","match":"Similar","desc":"A soft, dairy-free spread made from vegetable oils that can replace butter on toast and in some recipes.","where":"Refrigerated spreads section"},{"name_en":"Coconut oil","match":"Similar","desc":"A lactose-free fat that can stand in for butter in baking and sautéing, especially in recipes where a mild coconut note is acceptable.","where":"Cooking oils and baking aisle"},{"name_en":"Avocado oil spread","match":"Similar","desc":"A dairy-free spread made with avocado oil that offers a butter-like texture for everyday use.","where":"Refrigerated plant-based spreads section"}]}

column1: rice vinegar — general substitution for acidic condiment
column12: {"note":"Rice vinegar is a mild, slightly sweet acid used for dressings, marinades, and sushi rice, so these substitutes cover similar acidity with nearby flavor profiles.","alts":[{"name_en":"Seasoned rice vinegar","match":"Very close","desc":"Rice vinegar with added sugar and salt, giving the closest swap when you want the same mild tang with a bit of seasoning.","where":"International aisle or vinegar section"},{"name_en":"White wine vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"A light, clean vinegar with moderate acidity that works well in dressings, sauces, and quick pickles.","where":"Vinegar aisle"},{"name_en":"Champagne vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"A delicate vinegar with a softer profile than distilled vinegar, making it a good stand-in for recipes that need gentle acidity.","where":"Vinegar aisle or specialty foods section"},{"name_en":"Apple cider vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"A mildly fruity vinegar that can replace rice vinegar in many recipes, especially marinades and dressings.","where":"Vinegar aisle"}]}

column1: chicken breast + vegetarian — all alts must be meat-free
column12: {"note":"These vegetarian options can replace chicken breast in meals while staying fully meat-free.","alts":[{"name_en":"Extra-firm tofu","match":"Different but works","desc":"A versatile soy protein that soaks up marinades and can be sliced, cubed, or pan-seared like chicken.","where":"Refrigerated tofu section or natural foods case"},{"name_en":"Tempeh","match":"Different but works","desc":"A firm fermented soy product with a hearty, nutty texture that works well in stir-fries, sandwiches, and grain bowls.","where":"Refrigerated plant-protein section or natural foods case"},{"name_en":"Seitan","match":"Different but works","desc":"A wheat-gluten protein with a chewy, meat-like bite that is especially good for sautéing, grilling, or baking.","where":"Refrigerated vegan meat alternatives section or freezer aisle"},{"name_en":"Soy curls","match":"Different but works","desc":"Dried soy protein strips that rehydrate into a tender, chicken-like texture for skillet dishes, soups, and wraps.","where":"Dry goods aisle, natural foods section, or plant-based protein aisle"}]}

column1: fish sauce + Korean taste — cuisine-appropriate alternatives
column12: {"note":"Korean cooking often uses fish sauce for deep umami and saltiness, so these substitutes aim to keep the same savory profile in North American stores.","alts":[{"name_en":"Anchovy sauce","match":"Similar","desc":"Closest Korean-style substitute for fish sauce, with a strong savory seafood flavor used in kimchi, stews, and marinades.","where":"Asian aisle, Korean grocery section, or near sauces"},{"name_en":"Thai fish sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A direct fish sauce alternative with a similar salty, fermented umami taste that works well in most Korean recipes.","where":"Asian aisle or international sauces section"},{"name_en":"Vietnamese fish sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"Another standard fish sauce option that delivers the same fermented seafood depth and can replace fish sauce 1:1.","where":"Asian aisle or international sauces section"},{"name_en":"Soy sauce + a little anchovy extract or broth","match":"Different but works","desc":"A workable Korean-friendly backup that adds salt and umami, though it is less oceanic and more soy-forward than fish sauce.","where":"Condiments aisle plus Asian aisle"}]}
